data.title: As a team using DSO with Jira, removing a local relates_to link removes the corresponding Relates link in Jira
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add UNLINK event processing to bridge-outbound.py so that locally-removed relates_to relationships are also removed from Jira using a read-before-delete pattern.
**Why**: Without UNLINK sync, removing a local relationship leaves a stale link in Jira, creating confusion about which cross-epic coordination links are still active.
**Scope**:
- IN: UNLINK event handler in process_outbound, read-before-delete pattern (GET issue links, match by both endpoints, DELETE the specific link), 404 on source issue treated as "already gone" (not error), graceful degradation on Jira rejection
- OUT: LINK event handling (previous story), round-trip verification

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, an UNLINK event in the git diff stream causes bridge-outbound.py to remove the corresponding Jira link by reading existing links, matching both endpoints, and deleting the specific link
  <- Satisfies: "UNLINK events remove the corresponding Jira link using a read-before-delete pattern (GET issue links, match by both endpoints, DELETE the specific link)"
- When this story is complete, a 404 response on the source issue during UNLINK processing is treated as "already gone" and does not produce an error
  <- Satisfies: "Treat 404 on source issue as already gone (not error)"
- When this story is complete, if Jira rejects the link deletion, a bridge_alert is written and the sync run continues without blocking
  <- Satisfies: "Jira rejection handled gracefully: writes bridge_alert and persist_relationship_rejection, does not block local link creation or other sync operations"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Read-before-delete must match BOTH endpoints to avoid removing the wrong link
- [Reliability] Concurrent modifications between read and delete are possible -- handle gracefully with bridge_alert on 404/409
